channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Denver Broncos def. Atlanta Falcons 38-6
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Bo Nix made a statement Sunday, playing like a seasoned veteran despite his rookie status. The Broncos quarterback set career highs in passing yards, touchdowns, and completion percentage as Denver cruised to a decisive 38-6 victory over the Falcons.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: James Harden breaks tie with Ray Allen, moves to No. 2 on NBA's career 3-point list
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — James Harden now stands alone at No. 2 in 3-pointers. The Los Angeles Clippers guard connected on the 2,974th 3-pointer of his career Sunday night and broke a tie with Basketball Hall of Famer Ray Allen for the No. 2 spot on the NBA’s list.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Darius Garland scores 25, Cavaliers become 4th team to start 15-0 with 128-114 win over Hornets
item.description: Darius Garland scored 25 points, Ty Jerome matched his career-high with 24 in place of Donovan Mitchell and the Cleveland Cavaliers stayed unbeaten with a 128-114 win over the Charlotte Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Tom Withers, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NBA fines Bucks coach Doc Rivers for comments about call refs admitted they got wrong
item.description: Milwaukee coach Doc Rivers was fined $25,000 by the NBA on Sunday for public criticism of the officiating on a foul against Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo that the referees admitted they got wrong.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NBA fines Hornets star LaMelo Ball $100K for anti-gay comment during postgame interview
item.description: Charlotte Hornets do-it-all point guard LaMelo Ball was fined $100,000 for making an anti-gay comment during his postgame interview after beating the Milwaukee Bucks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harry Kane sparks England rout against Ireland and Nations League promotion
item.description: Harry Kane's 69th international goal sparked a second-half scoring spree that fired England back to the top tier of the UEFA Nations League on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas A&M to mark 25th anniversary of campus bonfire collapse that killed 12
item.description: The first, ominous sounds came from deep within the massive stack of logs in the darkest hours of the Texas night. Witnesses described hearing the stack of thousands of logs moan and creak before the crack of the center pole as it snapped, then collapsed.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia's Kirby Smart calls out CFP selection committee after win over Tennessee
item.description: Georgia Bulldogs head coach Kirby Smart delivered a direct message to the College Football Playoff selection committee on Saturday after a win over Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LeBron James puts Deion Sanders' critics on notice, says 'haters' are 'in hiding' after Colorado's latest win
item.description: Colorado was one of the most intriguing teams in college football last season, but they were not able to put together very many wins. The 2024 season has been much different.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utes get run over by #17 Colorado, 49-24
item.description: BOULDER, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utes losing streak continues, with no end in sight. Shedeur Sanders overcame a shaky start to throw three touchdown passes, LaJohntay Wester returned a punt 76 yards for a score and No. 18 Colorado pulled away late to beat Utah 49-24 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jake Paul, 27, beats 58-year-old Mike Tyson as the hits don't match the hype
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — The boos from a crowd wanting more action were growing again when Jake Paul dropped his gloves before the final bell and bowed toward 58-year-old Mike Tyson.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bird, Fowles, Pondexter, Beard headline Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025
item.description: WNBA greats Sue Bird, Sylvia Fowles, Cappie Pondexter and Alana Beard headline the Women's Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2025 that was announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Goalkeeper Olivia Bodmer leads Rutgers back to NCAA tournament
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – Rutgers women's soccer will host UConn in the opening round of the NCAA tournament on Saturday. This is the Scarlet Knights’ 13th consecutive appearance in the tournament, but how they got there was something Rutgers nation had never seen before.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Bountiful headed back to 5A championship game
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Bountiful Redhawks are headed back to the 5A championship game. Bountiful cruised past Brighton in the 5A semifinals Friday at Rice-Eccles Stadium, 24-8, and will take on Roy for the 5A state title next week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Former Sky coach Teresa Weatherspoon hired for new Unrivaled 3-on-3 basketball league
item.description: The total salary pool for the 36 players is more than $8 million, with players receiving equity in the league.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The world's tallest teenager, 7-foot-9 Olivier Rioux, plans to redshirt at Florida this season
item.description: GAINESVILLE, Fla. (AP) — The world’s tallest teenager might have to wait a year to become the world’s tallest college basketball player. Florida coach Todd Golden says Olivier Rioux, a 7-foot-9 freshman who owns a spot in the Guinness record book, is planning to redshirt.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mike Tyson concedes the role of villain to young foe in 58-year-old's fight with Jake Paul
item.description: Friendship is at the heart of how a fight came together between 58-year-old former heavyweight champion Mike Tyson and much younger YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jazz beat Dallas for first home victory, 115-113
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was finally home sweet home for the Utah Jazz. John Collins scored the last of his 28 points on a tiebreaking dunk with 6.4 seconds left to give the Utah Jazz their first home victory of the season, 115-113 over the Dallas Mavericks on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Curry upset after Klay 'ghosted' him before game, Draymond Green says
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- While it seemed to be all sunshine and roses inside Chase Center as the packed arena welcomed back Klay Thompson on Tuesday, that wasn't exactly the case -- at least amongst the players on the court.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Bucs TE Cade Otton emerging as reliable target for Baker Mayfield in absence of injured receivers
item.description: Cade Otton is showing the rest of the NFL the type of offensive weapon the Tampa Bay Buccaneers always felt he could become.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays to play 2025 season at Yankees' spring training field
item.description: The Tampa Bay Rays will play their 2025 home games at the New York Yankees' nearby spring training ballpark amid uncertainty about the future of hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field, Rays executives told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clippers write love letter to Los Angeles with new City Edition uniforms
item.description: With the NBA now in full swing and the brand-new Intuit Dome open for business, the Los Angeles Clippers have unveiled their City Edition uniforms for the new season. The new look, which the team says symbolizes the story and work of L.A.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Grand Prix of Long Beach sold ahead of iconic race's 50th edition
item.description: The Grand Prix of Long Long Beach, the longest-running major street circuit race in North America, will have a new owner heading into the 50th edition of the event next year. Penske Entertainment announced its purchase of Grand Prix Association of Long Beach, LLC, from longtime owner Gerald R.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Vejmelka makes 49 saves as Utah beats Carolina, 4-1
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club needed a victory in the worst way, and backup goalie Karel Vejmelka made sure they got one. Vejmelka made a career-high 49 saves, as Utah killed off over ten minutes of power plays in route to a 4-1 victory over the Carolina Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Finally healthy, Puka Nacua poised for strong finish
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - Puka Nacua was not supposed to be this good, this quickly. As a fifth round draft pick out of BYU by the L.A. Rams in 2023, Nacua took the NFL by storm, setting rookie records with 104 catches for 1,486 yards.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mitchell Chaffee's journey to the Tampa Bay Lightning
item.description: Making it to the NHL is a huge accomplishment for forward Mitchell Chaffee, especially as an undrafted player. Not only did he beat the odds, he’s overcome a serious injury.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Legendary Dodgers pitcher Fernando Valenzuela's cause of death revealed
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the legendary Los Angeles Dodgers pitcher, died of septic shock according to TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Want a Utah Hockey Club jersey? Circle this day on your calendar
item.description: Eager to get your hands on an inaugural Utah Hockey Club jersey? You'll want to circle Friday, Nov. 15.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: USC women’s basketball team beats Cal State Northridge by 85 points
item.description: The USC Trojans women’s basketball team was not messing around Tuesday night, shattering the team record for most points scored in a single game. Led by standout JuJu Watkins (21 points, nine assists, six steals), the Trojans beat the Matadors of Cal State Northridge by a score of 124-39.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Column: Coaching cannibalism of Chicago quarterbacks continues
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The dysfunction of the Chicago Bears coaching staff is plain for all to see, and it will only continue to hamper the development of the most talented and famous football player ever drafted by the franchise if left to continue as-is.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: 2024 Silver Pigskin Gala Awards
item.description: The Ballots for the 2024 Silver Pigskin Gala Awards have been released today to over 200 local coaches, PPR staff, former winners, local media, and influencers to vote on the best players in the county.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Utah athletic director fined $40,000 by Big 12 Conference
item.description: The Big 12 fine is considered stiff, as the three athletic director fines of note in the past decade were all $25,000.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, NewsNation

item.title: Dodger Stadium improvements could provide leg-up in team's pursuit of World Series repeat
item.description: When the 2025 MLB season begins next spring, the Los Angeles Dodgers will have a renovated clubhouse worthy of the defending World Series champions. Work has begun at Dodger Stadium, including renovations to the home team clubhouse, which houses team lockers, showers and communal areas.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5
